LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE APR-25-12 DENIAL OF CCP SECTION 1021.5 PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR. THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO DECIDE THIS MOTION DUE TO THE APPEAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON MAY 24, 2012. IF THE COURT HAD JURISDICTION TO DECIDE THE MOTION, THE MOTION WOULD BE DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT ANY NEW FACTS OR LAW TO DEMONSTRATE THAT HE WAS A SUCCESSFUL PARTY WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1021.5 OR THAT THIS LITIGATION SIGNIFICANTLY BENEFITTED THE GENERAL PUBLIC. REQUEST FOR SANCTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Jun-13-2012 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Reconsideration Of The Apr-25-12 Denial
Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - PATRICK MISSUD, TEL. NO. 584-7251, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. AMY L. FOSCALINA OF WOOD SMITH & BERMAN LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 222-3403 APPEARING FOR DR. HORTON.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S Motion For Reconsideration Of The Apr-25-12 Denial of CCP Section 1021.5 Private Attorney General's Motion IS Off calendar. This court lacks jurisdiction to decide this motion due to the appeal filed by plaintiff on May 24, 2012. If the court had jurisdiction to decide the motion, the motion would be denied because Plaintiff fails to present any new facts or law to demonstrate that he was a successful party within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 1021.5 or that this litigation significantly benefitted the general public. Request for sanction is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion Per Ccp 1021.5 "Private Attorney General Statute"; Memo P/A; Declaration
Off Calendar Apr-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S MOTION PER CCCP 1021.5 "PRIVATE ATTORNEY GENERAL STATUTE."
Appearances: Patrick Missud (584.7251) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; Amy L. Foscalina of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (925.222.3400) for defendant and responding party DR Horton.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that he is entitled to private attorney general fees. Plaintiff did not prevail in this action and the case did not significantly benefit the general public. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Apr-11-2012 Denied

Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Allow Lab Investments, Llc To Intervene In This Action To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Mar-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph S. May, Esq., of the Law Office of Joseph S. May, Tel. 415.781.3333, appearing for Intervenor and moving party.
Patrick Missud, Tel. 415.584.7251, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR LAB INVESTMENTS, LLC'S Motion To Allow Lab Investments, LLC To Intervene In This Action, To Expunge Lis Pendens and for attorneys fees and costs is Granted. LAB Investments, LLC is granted leave to intervene, the lis pendens is expunged and Mr. Missud must pay $4,030 to LAB Investments, LLC. LAB Investments, LLC has shown that it is the owner of the property, there is no real property claim concerning the property that has been filed in this action nor, even if there were, has Mr. Missud established the probable validity of such a claim. Mr. Missud's filing of the lis pendens and his refusal to withdraw it was without substantial justification. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Otion For Reconsideation Of Jan-19-11 Motion To Vacate Sister State Judgemnt Per Ccp 1710.10
Off Calendar Jun-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF JANUARY 19, 2011 MOTION TO VACATE SISTER STATE JUDGMENT PER CCP SECTION 1710.10 ET SEQ.

APPEARANCES: Patrick Missud, 584-7251 pltf & moving party in propria persona; Amy L. Foscalina, Esq. of Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman LLP 925/356-8217 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. In addition, the Court denied the request for sanctions. Order signed. Defendant also submitted a proposed order re 03/23/11 hearing re granting stay/requiring undertaking. Order signed after hearing. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Per Ccp Sections 916 Et Seq., And 1021 Et Seq; Request For Oral Testimony
Off Calendar Mar-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS PATRICK MISSUD, JULIE MISSUD'S MOTION FOR STAY PER CCP SECTIONS 916 ET SEQ., AND 1021 ET SEQ.

Appearances: Joel Odou of Wood Smith Henning & Berman (702.498.2134) for defendant and responding party; Patrick Missud (584.7251) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Judgment stayed pending final resolution of the appeal from the sister state judgment in Nevada. CCP 1710.50 (a)(1). Plaintiff to post an undertaking for the full amount of the judgment: $48,691.97. CCP 1710.50 (c)(1). Defendant's evidentiary objections are sustained. Plaintiff's request for oral testimony is denied. Plaintiff to post the undertaking within 10 days from notice of entry of order. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
